The post US-Iran talks in Pakistan end after 21 hours with no deal; US negotiators leave appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Thousands protest government and Iran war in Tel Aviv, exceeding court-ordered limits
description:
At least 2,000 people estimated at Habima Square rally, double the permitted number; heavy police presence, but no violence or arrests; protests also take place in Jerusalem, Haifa
The post Thousands protest government and Iran war in Tel Aviv, exceeding court-ordered limits appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Trump shares article saying he could impose naval blockade on Iran if it won’t ‘bend’
description:
Bennett announces first two members of his party's slate for upcoming Knesset elections * Polls open in Hungary in vote that could put end to Orban's 16-year rule

title: NORAD’s F-16s Practice Air Defense Operations from Remote Alaskan Airfield
description: F-16s of the 18th Fighter Interceptor Squadron were deployed to the remote King Salmon airport to demonstrate NORAD’s readiness to respond to potential threats throughout Alaska. U.S. Air Force F-16 Fighting Falcons assigned to the 18th Fighter Interceptor Squadron (18th FIS) were deployed by the North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD) from Eielson AFB to []

title: US Central Command denies IRGC missile strikes on USS Tripoli
description:
US CENTCOM has denied claims from Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps that the amphibious assault ship USS Tripoli was hit by missiles.
The post US Central Command denies IRGC missile strikes on USS Tripoli  appeared first on Naval Technology.

title: US special forces rescue second F-15 airman from Iran
description: The airman, the weapons officer of an F-15 jet shot down on Friday, was wounded but “will be just fine,” President Trump announced.

title: Trump again chides NATO for failing to back US operations in Iran
description: The president's remarks came just a day after a tense private meeting with the alliance's Secretary General Mark Rutte at the White House.

title: More than 500 people arrested at Palestine Action rally in London
description: Police in London arrested more than 500 people at a pro-Palestinian demonstration on Saturday, as activists gathered in Trafalgar Square to show support for the banned group Palestine Action, in a mass protest that has intensified a debate over civil liberties and the right to dissent.

title: Middle East war live: US and Iran fail to reach deal after marathon peace talks in Pakistan
description: US Vice President JD Vance said on Sunday that 21 hours of peace talks in Pakistan between the US and Iran had failed to produce an agreement to end the war. Each side blamed the other for the failure of the negotiations. Read our live blog below to follow the day's events as they unfold.

title: Hungarians vote in landmark election closely watched by EU, Russia, US, Israel
description:
Orban, in power since 2010, faces voter discontent over stagnant economy; rival Magyar ahead in polls, but outcome up in the air; Israeli diplomat endorses Orban on X, then deletes post
The post Hungarians vote in landmark election closely watched by EU, Russia, US, Israel appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Vance heads to high-stakes Iran talks: "JD is going to the Super Bowl"
description: Vice President Vance left Friday for Pakistan and the biggest challenge of his career: negotiating a deal with Iran to solve the nuclear dispute and end the war."This is a big deal for JD. He is going to the Super Bowl," one U.S. official told Axios.Why it matters: While the meeting itself is historic — the most high-level engagement between U.S. and Iranian officials since 1979 — the chances for success appear low. title: Trump looms large over Republican congressional primaries
description: President Trump will not be on the ballot this year, but you might not know it based on the ads being run by Republicans in competitive congressional primaries.Why it matters: Even as his approval rating droops amid rising gas prices and a sluggish economy, the president is still one of the most potent forces in Republican politics.His endorsement is highly coveted by Republican congressional candidates, and the threat of his ire and anti-endorse

title: Hungarians vote in a key election that could unseat populist Prime Minister Orbán
description: Hungarians headed to the polls on Sunday in a pivotal parliamentary election that could end Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s 16-year grip on power, as the opposition led by Peter Magyar challenges his nationalist rule in a contest drawing scrutiny across Europe and backing from US President Donald Trump.

title: Pakistan urges US and Iran to uphold ceasefire after talks end
description: Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has insisted the US and Iran must uphold their ceasefire agreement.

title: Oil tankers exit Strait of Hormuz amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire
description: Three supertankers laden with oil exit waterway amid global energy crunch, shipping data shows.

title: Why did US-Iran talks end without an agreement in Pakistan?
description: Despite high-level talks for the first time in years, the US and Iran couldnt reach agreement on terms to end the war.
